title: ExecutionFork
description: A delta execution environment that overlays changed workloads, local mappings, and isolation adapters onto a shared baseline. This is the canonical contract for lightweight forked runtime surfaces.

specVersion.description: Spec version of this document, e.g. "2.0.0".

description.description: Optional explanation of why the fork exists.

substrate.description: Primary substrate used to realize this fork.

baselineRef.description: Reference to the inherited baseline environment, release, or surface.

baselineDigest.description: Optional digest of the resolved baseline state.

routingContractRef.description: Routing contract that selects and steers traffic into this fork.

runnerGroupRef.description: Optional default runner group for validations executed against this fork.

capabilityPolicyRef.description: Optional governing capability policy for actions inside this fork.

quotaPolicyRef.description: Optional quota policy applied to this fork.

isolationRefs.description: References to isolation adapters or data partitioning resources used by the fork.

workloads.description: Changed workloads or local overlays attached to the fork.

targetRef.description: Target workload or service being forked.

writePolicy.description: Whether writes inherit baseline behavior, remain isolated, or are blocked.

envOverrides.description: Environment overrides to apply for this workload.

ttl.description: Advisory lifetime for this fork, e.g. "8h".

cleanupPolicy.description: Cleanup behavior once the fork expires or is explicitly removed.

tags.description: Classification tags for actor, mission, trust class, or workflow.
